metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2447
metadata.comment:
Rationale: This works utilizes the transgenic expression of human plastin 3 on a genetic background that is knock out for the SMN protein. The SMN is responsible for spinal muscular atrophy which sees early death and an associated disruption in the organization of the neuromuscular junction. This disrupts the size of the presynaptic terminal vesicle cluster and active zone (Figures 9A-H); leads to a EM quantified disruption of docked vesicles (10 A and B); and in transmitter release based on quantal content and ready releasable pool size (11A-C). All of these synaptic disruptions a rescued by the expression of recombinant expressed human plastin). These disruption in synaptic parameters are all rescued by the plastin 3 overexpression to supporting a significance for synaptic function.. Experimental description: Fig.9: "PLS3V5 effects on presynaptic vesicle content, AZ number and F-actin caging of mixed50 SMA mice."
Fig.10: "Electron micrographs of NMJs showing restored docked or fused SVs in PLS3V5 mixed50 SMA mice."
